What's going on everyone? So financial metrics for retail, this is going to be probably one of the shortest videos we have and that's because it's really, really simple because most of these things are already calculated for you. So net revenue is going to be total revenue minus refunds. This is already what is reported to you because that is the only useful number. Total revenue is kind of just for your ego. Net revenue is going to be what actually was sold and collected after refunds, etc. All right. Profit is going to be your commissions earned. So especially if you're doing a zero inventory type model, which I highly recommend because it is the model of the future, you're going to have your commissions earned, right? And so that is your profit. So it's pretty straightforward. Margin percentage. All you have to do here is just divide how much you earned in commissions divided by your net revenue. The reason that this is important is that it will show you if you were selling the highest margin products and you can, this is a stat that you can push your team on and be like, hey guys, we're at 35% and I want to set 42 or 44, right? These are the products that are going to push us over that level. So let's push these packages more than these ones, etc. And so that way you can guide your team, put their attention on the things that are going to actually drive the most revenue in the business or really most profit in the business, excuse me, by just having that number. So that's the reason there's only three very straightforward, but it's good to track these things so that you know what's going on and what you can focus on.
